paragraphs.text: “And there was a strife between the herdmen of Abram’s cattle and the herdmen of Lot’s cattle” (Gen 13:7). For this, Lot’s quarrelsome servants were by God’s justice sent to the Sodomites, who were also quarrelsome, that with them they too might bear the punishment, while Lot God plucked out from their midst. Although God promised that land to Abram, He allowed Lot to choose for himself the land by the Jordan, that is, all the land of Sodom through which the Jordan flowed.

After Lot had separated from Abram, God appeared to Abram and said to him: “Arise, walk through the land in the length and in the breadth of it; for I will give it unto thee” (Gen 13:17). By this is clearly foretokened the Cross. But the land promised to the forefathers in the mystery of the Cross, for the Cross’ sake cast out her children, and those lands have been given to other heirs.
